title: The river clean-up
commandDescriptor: Make an idea clear by giving a point, evidence and explanation.
skillFocus: Explaining using evidence from the text
answerPattern: Answer stem → point → evidence → explanation → link back
teachingTip: After giving evidence, add a sentence that explains what the evidence proves.
text: Before the clean-up, plastic bags clung to the reeds and no one sat near the riverbank. By the end of Saturday, the volunteers had filled twelve sacks with rubbish. On Monday, two ducks returned to the water, and families spread picnic blankets under the willow trees.
textHtml: Before the clean-up, plastic bags clung to the reeds and no one sat near the riverbank. By the end of Saturday, the volunteers had filled twelve sacks with rubbish. On Monday, two ducks returned to the water, and families spread picnic blankets under the willow trees.

weakExplanation: This gives one detail from the text, but it does not explain how the riverbank improves.
whatQuestionTheWeakAnswerWouldAnswer: How much rubbish did the volunteers collect?

Before the clean-up, plastic bags clung to the reeds and no one sat nearby, but afterwards ducks return to the water and families have picnics there. This shows that removing the rubbish makes the riverbank a place that living things can use and enjoy again.
